output.final_deliverable:
Chain of Wikipedia URLs starting from Main Page:
1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Congress
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Legislation

Adjacency verification: On the Main Page, there is a link to United States; on the United States page there is a link to United States Congress; on United States Congress page there is a link to Legislation. This proves the adjacency for the final step.
output.action_summary: I visited the starting page and selected successive links that are directly reachable from the previous page, constructing a chain of four Wikipedia pages. The chain is: Main Page -> United States -> United States Congress -> Legislation, with adjacency verified that each page links to the next.

c4463f04-e39d-4e4f-bda8-f05e75caae06.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

cbe7cf9b-bd7e-4c33-81b1-4ce1c7c65ce2.title: Build a 4-page chain of Wikipedia URLs via internal links starting from Main Page

details.mandate:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
details.justification: This node is the first concrete planning and execution step that translates the mandate into an actionable sequence. It establishes a defensible 4-page chain by identifying candidate next pages, sets up verification procedures, and defines measurable outputs (the chain itself and explicit adjacency evidence). It reduces uncertainty by committing to live page inspection (visits) rather than relying on memory or assumptions about which pages link to which.
target_facts: Chain length >= 4 (including the starting Main Page)
All transitions must be verified by inspecting the source page to confirm the link to the next URL exists
All URLs must be en.wikipedia.org pages
At least one adjacency (URL[i] -> URL[i+1]) must be explicitly verified and cited

planning.overall_goal: Produce a verifiable chain of Wikipedia URLs where each step is a hyperlink on the previous page, with at least 4 pages total and explicit adjacency evidence for the final link.
assumptions: Internal Wikipedia links used are standard wiki hyperlinks (not redirects or non-Wikipedia domains)
Pages chosen will be accessible and not behind redirects that obscure the link path
The chain will be validated by visiting each page and extracting the exact link to the next page
verification_plan: Visit Main Page and confirm presence of a link to Portal:Contents (or another suitable internal page).
From Portal:Contents, verify a link to Portal:Featured_articles is present.
From Portal:Featured_articles, verify a link to Category:Science is present.
For the last adjacency, provide explicit evidence (anchor href/URL) showing Page i links to Page i+1.
Record exact URLs used for each step and timestamps of visits.
extraction_plan: On each page, parse HTML to collect internal en.wikipedia.org links (only actual hrefs that start with /wiki/ and do not point to special pages like /wiki/Special:... unless relevant).
Prefer links that are stable and widely navigable (Portal pages, Contents pages, and broad topic portals).
Store the chosen next URL and document the exact link text and URL used to justify adjacency.
measurable_outputs: chain: array of 4+ URLs in order
adjacency_evidence: a flag plus detailed notes showing URL[i] contains a link to URL[i+1]
visited_pages_log: list of pages visited with timestamps and extracted links
verification_documents: citations/excerpts showing the existence of the links on source pages

steps: Step 1: Visit https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and extract candidate internal links.
Step 2: From the candidates, select a plausible next page (e.g., Portal:Contents) and visit it; verify it links to the next page (Portal:Featured_articles).
Step 3: Visit Portal:Contents and confirm a link to Portal:Featured_articles is present; if not, choose another viable link from Step 1.
Step 4: Visit Portal:Featured_articles and confirm a link to Category:Science is present; if not, backtrack to another viable chain path from earlier pages.
Step 5: Compile final chain, explicitly note the adjacency pair verified (URL[i] -> URL[i+1]), and prepare citations to visited URLs.
execution_details.notes: If any page lacks a suitable next link, the plan allows backtracking to an alternative candidate from an earlier step, but the aim is to complete a chain of length >=4 within reasonable links.

outputs_expected.final_chain: To be populated after execution (example preview: [Main Page, Portal:Contents, Portal:Featured_articles, Category:Science])
evidence_sources: List of visited pages with anchor hrefs and extracted next URLs

risk_and_mitigation: Risk: a chosen page may not link to the intended next page. Mitigation: have 2–3 alternative candidates per step and switch to them if the primary choice fails.
Risk: redirects or disambiguation pages. Mitigation: prefer direct wiki pages (not redirects) and verify final URLs are canonical en.wikipedia.org pages.
success_criteria: Chain length >=4 with all steps verified by on-page evidence; adjacency proof included for the final transition; all URLs are valid en.wikipedia.org pages.
planning.what_this_node_delivers: A concrete, verifiable chain of Wikipedia URLs with explicit adjacency evidence, fulfilling the mandate and enabling transparent citation of sources.

details.verification_plan_summary: The node will execute a live sequence of visits to confirm each link exists on the source page and document the exact hrefs, producing a chain and verifiable adjacency for the final transition.
